key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 diluted EPS $38.06 less estimated reserve-release and peak-cycle normalization; owner cash $30.0/sh on 267.1M shares
Mid-single-digit premium growth, combined ratio normalizes near 92-94%, buybacks offset share count; 11x exit on year-10 owner cash

open_questions: Validate normalized owner cash **$30.00** per share versus company-adjusted metrics in next earnings materials
Confirm stance: registry **watch** vs Marvin-proposed **hold** on 19.82% synthesis IRR
FY2025 peak ROE **42.3%** and combined ratio **85.2%** may not persist through the cycle
No third-party sources indexed; re-run scan when Substacks or fund letters are added
thesis: The Allstate Corporation is one of the largest publicly held U.S. personal-lines property and casualty insurers. **[Fact]** It sells auto, homeowners, and other personal insurance through exclusive agents, independent agents, contact centers, and direct channels under the Allstate, National General, Direct Auto, and Answer Financial brands. **[Fact]** At year-end 2025 the company reported **211 million** policies in force, **$59.5 billion** of Property-Liability premiums written, and an **$83.24 billion** investment portfolio (`ALL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260220_rpt20251231_acc0000899051_26_000031.htm`, Item 1 Business). Protection Services (roadside, protection plans, dealer services, identity protection, and Arity telematics) and a Run-off Property-Liability segment round out the group. This is an underwriting and investment compounder, not a balance-sheet-free croupier.
why_market_wrong: After the 2022-2024 auto and homeowners loss-inflation shock, Allstate repriced aggressively and cut expenses; the Property-Liability combined ratio fell to **85.2%** in 2025 from **104.5%** in 2023. **[Fact]** **[Inference]** The market may still overweight catastrophe headlines and the 2022-2023 earnings collapse while under-crediting durable pricing power, Transformative Growth distribution, and a **$4 billion** share-repurchase authorization announced for 2025-2026. **[Inference]** Conversely, FY2025 GAAP diluted earnings of **$38.06** per share and **42.3%** return on equity embed favorable prior-year reserve releases (**$1.81 billion**, 3.1 points on the combined ratio) and a peak-cycle underwriting margin; normalized owner cash is lower than spot GAAP earnings. No dated equity-yield-curve catalyst; return is franchise quality at a normalized earnings power.
